2026-04-17T16:24:20.9421967Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T16:24:20.9422410Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T16:24:20.9422774Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T16:24:20.9423088Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T16:24:20.9423329Z [36;1m[0m

If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README for 1.6.0-beta.1 new features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57510 / commit 8ba2ed9 by `@azure-sdk` (version bumped to 1.6.0-beta.2 in #58296)\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57510 upgraded the API version to `2026-01-15-preview` and introduced several new types and properties:\\n\\n- New **`FoundryAutoUpgrade`** model – configures auto-upgrade behavior for AI Foundry accounts (fields: `Mode`, `PlannedByMicrosoft`, `StatusReason`, `ScheduledOn`)\\n- New **`FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`** enum (`Enabled`, `Disabled`, etc.)\\n- New **`CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`** enum\\n- **`CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`** gained three new properties: `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, `DefaultProject`\\n- **`CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource`** received ~100 lines of new operations\\n- `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage` each gained new properties\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `1.6.0-beta.1` CHANGELOG entry only says _\\u0026#34;Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview\\u0026#34;_ – none of the new types (`FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`, `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`) or new properties on existing models are mentioned\\n- `README.md` **Examples** section contains only a link to general management samples; there are no package-specific code snippets for creating/managing a Cognitive Services account\\n- `README.md` **Key concepts** section links to general Azure SDK docs but does not list or describe the resource types surfaced by this package (accounts, deployments, projects, connections, RAI policies, managed network settings, etc.)\\n- None of the AI Foundry-related features added since v1.5.0 (Projects, Connections, CapabilityHosts) are described or demonstrated in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1 – `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.6.0-beta.1` section to list the new types and properties:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to `2026-01-15-preview`.\\n- Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade` model and `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode` enum to configure AI Foundry auto-upgrade behavior on a Cognitive Services account.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType` enum for quota scope classification.\\n- Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, and `DefaultProject` properties to `CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`.\\n- Enhanced `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` with additional management operations.\\n- Added new properties to `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage`.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.53.0.\\n- Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2 – `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md`\\n\\nUpdate the **Key concepts** section to enumerate resource types, and replace the generic **Examples** section with a concrete snippet placeholder.\\n\\nReplace the current `## Key concepts` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` library manages the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `CognitiveServicesAccountResource` | Top-level Cognitive Services account |\\n| `CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource` | Model deployment within an account |\\n| `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` | AI Foundry project scoped under an account |\\n| `CognitiveServicesConnectionResource` | Connection attached to an account or project |\\n| `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource` | Capability host attached to an account |\\n| `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` | Managed virtual network settings for an account |\\n| `RaiPolicyResource` / `RaiBlocklistResource` | Responsible AI policy and blocklist resources |\\n| `CommitmentPlanResource` | Commitment plan for reserved capacity |\\n```\\n\\nReplace the current `## Examples` section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Cognitive Services account\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n(The snippet tooling will fill in the code once a sample test is written – see Step 2.)\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test file at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateCognitiveServicesAccount()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount\\n            // Authenticate and get a subscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // Get (or create) a resource group\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;)).Value;\\n\\n            // Define account properties\\n            var accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                Kind = \\u0026#34;OpenAI\\u0026#34;,\\n                Sku = new CognitiveServicesSku(\\u0026#34;S0\\u0026#34;),\\n                Properties = new CognitiveServicesAccountProperties\\n                {\\n                    CustomSubDomainName = \\u0026#34;my-openai-account\\u0026#34;,\\n                    PublicNetworkAccess = ServiceAccountPublicNetworkAccess.Enabled,\\n                }\\n            };\\n\\n            // Create the account\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts();\\n            ArmOperation operation =\\n                await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myAccount\\u0026#34;, accountData);\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created account: {account.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nPlace the snippet placeholder in `README.md` as shown in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently contains all required sections but some are thin:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Links to generic docs – needs package-specific resource list |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Links to generic samples – needs at least one inline snippet |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun in order – each must succeed before proceeding:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into README placeholders; verify with `git diff`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — only needed if public API surface changed (it did not change in this doc-only update)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign
